testCases.name: test_case_0
testCases.input:
Relationship: Partner
Answer 1: I love when she supports me and encourages me to keep going forward even when I am down. I love the way she helps me to do things that I am scared of doing. She is always there when I am very low and I need her.
Answer 2: I've seen her getting stressed. I've seen her worrying about things that are so obviously easy to figure out for me. I've also seen her in her most beautiful, intimate moments. I've seen her being shy. I've seen her naked. I've seen her happy about life, being playful.
Answer 3: I wanted to know that I really love her and appreciate her. Even though we might have fights and drift apart temporarily, we will come back together stronger.
Sender: Harshit
testCases.actualOutput:
Dear Partner,

I've been thinking lately about how much you hold me up without even trying. There are moments when the weight of everything feels too heavy, when I'm down or facing something that honestly scares me, and you are consistently the one who nudges me forward. You have this quiet way of making me feel capable when I feel small, and I want you to know that your presence during my lowest points isn't just "support"—it is the reason I'm able to keep going.

Because I get to see every side of you, I noticed that the world doesn't always see the girl I know. I see you get stressed over things that seem so simple to me, watching your mind work through worries, and it only makes me want to protect you more. But I also get the gift of seeing you at your most unguarded—the way you look when you're being playful and light, the soft shyness that still surfaces, and the breathtaking intimacy of you just being yourself with me. I've seen you at your most vulnerable and your most beautiful, and I am in awe of all of it.

I know we aren't perfect. We have our days where we fight or feel like we're drifting into our own separate worlds for a moment. But I want you to know, deeply and certainly, that those drifts are only temporary. We always find our way back, and every time we do, we are stronger for it. I love you more than I probably say, and I appreciate every version of you—the worried version, the brave version, and the one that is mine.

With all my love,

Harshit
testCases.expectedOutput: A warm, personal letter starting with 'Dear' that references all three input themes, signs off with 'Yours completely,' followed by 'Harshit', is 3-4 paragraphs, and contains no em dashes.
context: I love when she supports me and encourages me to keep going forward even when I am down. I love the way she helps me to do things that I am scared of doing. She is always there when I am very low and I need her.

I've seen her getting stressed. I've seen her worrying about things that are so obviously easy to figure out for me. I've also seen her in her most beautiful, intimate moments. I've seen her being shy. I've seen her naked. I've seen her happy about life, being playful.
I wanted to know that I really love her and appreciate her. Even though we might have fights and drift apart temporarily, we will come back together stronger.
retrievalContext: I love when she supports me and encourages me to keep going forward even when I am down. I love the way she helps me to do things that I am scared of doing. She is always there when I am very low and I need her.

I've seen her getting stressed. I've seen her worrying about things that are so obviously easy to figure out for me. I've also seen her in her most beautiful, intimate moments. I've seen her being shy. I've seen her naked. I've seen her happy about life, being playful.
I wanted to know that I really love her and appreciate her. Even though we might have fights and drift apart temporarily, we will come back together stronger.

testCases.name: test_case_1
testCases.input:
Relationship: Mom
Answer 1: She sends me a good morning text before I even wake up, every single day without fail.
Answer 2: The way she laughs when something genuinely surprises her, completely unguarded, head back, no performance at all.
Answer 3: That I notice everything. The effort she puts in quietly, the ways she shows up without being asked. I see it all.
Sender: Harshit
testCases.actualOutput:
Dear Mom,

Every morning I wake up to your text already waiting for me, and I don't think you know what that does to my heart. Before the world even stirs, before my feet hit the floor, you're already there. It's been every single day, and somehow you never miss. In a world that feels so uncertain, you've become my most reliable constant. That simple good morning carries the weight of your love, and I feel held by it before I'm even fully awake.

I've been thinking about your laugh lately. Not the polite one you give when someone tells a joke that isn't funny, but the real one. The one that comes out when something catches you completely off guard. Your whole face changes, your head tips back, and for those few seconds you're just pure joy with no filter, no editing. It's the most beautiful sound I know, and every time I hear it, I'm reminded of who you are underneath all the ways you take care of everyone else.

I need you to know that I notice everything. The way you quietly refold the towels after I've done laundry because you know I tried but didn't get them quite right. How you show up to things that matter to me even when you're exhausted. The small sacrifices you make that you think go unseen. They don't. I see the effort in your everyday kindness, the intention behind your care. I see how you love not just with words but with your whole life, and I'm so grateful to be on the receiving end of that kind of devotion.

You've shaped who I am in ways I'm still discovering. Thank you for being exactly who you are.

Harshit
testCases.expectedOutput: A warm, personal letter starting with 'Dear' that references all three input themes, signs off with 'With all my love,' followed by 'Harshit', is 3-4 paragraphs, and contains no em dashes.
context: She sends me a good morning text before I even wake up, every single day without fail.
The way she laughs when something genuinely surprises her, completely unguarded, head back, no performance at all.
That I notice everything. The effort she puts in quietly, the ways she shows up without being asked. I see it all.
retrievalContext: She sends me a good morning text before I even wake up, every single day without fail.
The way she laughs when something genuinely surprises her, completely unguarded, head back, no performance at all.
That I notice everything. The effort she puts in quietly, the ways she shows up without being asked. I see it all.

testCases.name: test_case_2
testCases.input:
Relationship: Mom
Answer 1: she takes care of her family and protects them from uncertainty
Answer 2: struggling in doing many things at once but never complaining
Answer 3: that i love her and am very grateful for all that she does for us.
Sender: Harshit
testCases.actualOutput:
Dear Mom,

I watch you every day, the way you wrap our family in this invisible shield of security, making sure we never have to worry about tomorrow while you carry all those concerns yourself. You have this remarkable ability to turn chaos into calm, to make our home feel safe even when the world outside feels uncertain. The way you anticipate what we need before we even know we need it, the way you plan and prepare and protect us from life's sharp edges. It's something I'm only now beginning to understand the weight of.

I see how much you juggle, how your hands are always busy with something for someone else. The meals that appear when we're hungry, the problems that get solved before they become our problems, the countless small acts that keep our lives running smoothly. You do all of this while managing your own struggles, your own tired moments, your own overwhelming days. And yet I rarely hear you complain. You just keep going, keep giving, keep holding us all together.

I need you to know how deeply grateful I am. Not just for the things you do, but for who you are. For the way you love us so completely that protecting us has become as natural as breathing. For the strength you show us every day, even when I know you don't feel strong. You've given me something invaluable by showing me what unconditional love looks like in action, and I carry that with me everywhere I go.

With all my love,
Harshit
testCases.expectedOutput: A warm, personal letter starting with 'Dear' that references all three input themes, signs off with 'With all my love,' followed by 'Harshit', is 3-4 paragraphs, and contains no em dashes.

struggling in doing many things at once but never complaining
that i love her and am very grateful for all that she does for us.

struggling in doing many things at once but never complaining
that i love her and am very grateful for all that she does for us.

testCases.name: test_case_3
testCases.input:
Relationship: Her
Answer 1: She thinks about small things related to me and let me know if that's right or wrong
Answer 2: Noticing her when she is busy and admiring how beautiful she is
Answer 3: She is a beautiful thing happened to me
Sender: Harshit
testCases.actualOutput:
Dear Shona,

There's something about the way you notice the smallest details about me that takes my breath away. You'll ask if I've eaten, or mention something I said weeks ago, or gently correct me when I'm about to make the same mistake twice. It's in these tiny moments that I realize how deeply you see me, how much space I occupy in your thoughts even when we're apart. No one has ever paid attention to me the way you do, and it makes me feel more known than I thought possible.

I find myself watching you when you're completely absorbed in something, when you don't even realize I'm there. The way you concentrate, the little expressions that cross your face, the grace in your movements even when you're rushing through your day. You're so beautiful in these unguarded moments, and I'm struck by this overwhelming tenderness, this quiet awe that you exist in my world at all.

I keep thinking about how some people spend their whole lives searching for something extraordinary, not realizing that extraordinary can arrive quietly, in the form of a person who changes everything simply by being themselves. You happened to me like that. Not with fanfare or drama, but like the most natural thing in the world, like you were always meant to be here. You are the most beautiful thing that has ever happened to me, and I'm still learning how to hold something so precious without being afraid of breaking it.

With everything I have,
Harshit
testCases.expectedOutput: A warm, personal letter starting with 'Dear' that references all three input themes, signs off with 'With everything I have,' followed by 'Harshit', is 3-4 paragraphs, and contains no em dashes.

testCases.name: test_case_4
testCases.input:
Relationship: Friend
Answer 1: She randomly checks in. Just "how are you" texts out of nowhere and following up on things she shouldn't even remember like when I told her about my academic registry stuff
Answer 2: We went for 2024 and it was such an experience. The way she was so happy. The way she just exploded with joy.
Answer 3: I wanna tell her that I'm grateful. For being there. For leaning in her faith in God to lead her. For being a friend I never knew I needed.
Sender: Owuraku
testCases.actualOutput:
Dear Michelle,

The way you've woven yourself into the fabric of my life still catches me off guard in the most beautiful way. Your random check-ins arrive exactly when I need them, like you have some sixth sense for when life gets overwhelming. I think about you taking the time to follow up on something as mundane as my academic registry stuff, and it strikes me how naturally thoughtfulness flows from you. You make being open feel safe, and conversations with you feel like coming home to a friend who truly gets it.

That explosion of joy at the 2024 experience with you by my side remains one of those crystalline memories I return to when I need to smile. Watching you experience that moment was its own kind of gift. But what moves me even more is how you navigate the harder seasons. The way you lean into your faith when everything else feels uncertain, how you let God lead even when the path isn't clear, it's something rare and beautiful to witness. Your faith isn't performative or loud, it's simply woven into who you are.

I keep trying to find adequate words for what our relationship means to me, but gratitude feels too small and love feels too vast. You've been support and laughter and wisdom wrapped up in one extraordinary person. The ease between us, this trust we've built, it's become one of the things I treasure most. Thank you for being exactly who you are, Michelle.

With so much warmth,
Owuraku (or as you would say it Kuu)

I wanna tell her that I'm grateful. For being there. For leaning in her faith in God to lead her. For being a friend I never knew I needed.
